name: S&P 500
summary: Day 63 Thursday — S&P 7,135.95 FLAT (-0.0%, NASDAQ +0.0%) ON FOMC HAWKISH-DISSENT + MAG 7 BEAT-AND-RAISE; VIX +5.5% TO 18.81 (FIRST-DAY REVERSAL OF -41.4% 1M EXTREME-COMPLACENCY TREND); SMH +1.7% RECOVERING APR 28 -3.0% SELLOFF. S&P 500 closed Wed Apr 29 at 7,138.80 (-0.5% 1D, +12.1% 1M); NASDAQ 24,663.80 (-0.9% 1D, +17.7% 1M); DOW 49,141.93 (-0.1%); VIX 17.93 (+0.6% 1D, -41.4% 1M); SMH 491.21 (-3.0% 1D, +31.3% 1M); URA 54.23 (-4.4% 1D); XLE 57.71 (+1.7% 1D on oil bid); Cheniere LNG 264.98 (+2.2%); Exxon 150.56 (+1.6%). DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER: WSJ Tue Apr 28 reported OpenAI MISSED Q1 INTERNAL TARGETS — 1B WAU goal, actual ~900M; CFO Sarah Friar told leadership she is worried about ability to fund future compute contracts if revenue doesn't accelerate. AI-spend-trajectory thesis cracks: if OpenAI (the demand anchor for Nvidia/AMD/Broadcom hyperscaler-scale orders) slows, the entire AI-capex stack reprices. SMH -3.0% biggest down-day since Mar 30; KOSPI +0.8% Wed DECOUPLED (Korean memory/HBM has its own cycle); TAIEX -0.6% modest pullback. Crisis resolving does NOT fix OpenAI's revenue miss — SEPARATE entry signal independent of war's resolution. MAG 7 EARNINGS TONIGHT (after Wed close): Microsoft Q3 (Azure growth, OpenAI deal revenue cap), Meta (capex $115-135B), Alphabet ($175-185B capex), Amazon ($200B capex). These four reports are the NEXT-24-HOUR BINARY DETERMINANT for AI-capex narrative. Microsoft-OpenAI partnership restructured Apr 27 (Microsoft no longer shares revenue with OpenAI) adds governance uncertainty. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
